Dear Mr. Kivi:
You have requested advice concerning the lobbying disclosure
obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local l-2 und.er
the PoliticaL Reform Act.l, During our teLephone conversation of
April 23, L99O, you stated that you are not authorized by the
American Federation of Musicians Local 12 to seek this advice. In
that case, v/e must decline to issue advice to you because the
Commission is prohibited fron providing advice to a third party
about the duties or obligations of another person or entity.
(Section 83114 r' Regulation f8329, copy enclosed.) However, I hope
the following general inforrnation will be of assistance.
The term trlobbyist" is defined in Section 82039 as follows:
rrLobbyistrr means any individual who is employed or contracts
for econonic consideration, other than reimbursement for
reasonable travel expenses, to cornmunicate directly or
through his or her agents with any el.ective state official,
agency official 
or legislative official 
for the purpose of
influencing legislative or adrninistrative action, if a
substantial or reqular portion of the activities 
for which he
or she receives consideration is for the purpose of
influencinq leqislative or adninistrative action.
1 Government code section 8100o-9101-5. AIl statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
Conrnission
regutations appear at 2 california code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et seq. AI1 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 5 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 O Sacramento CA 95804-0807 o (916) lll-5660


John Kivi
page 2
Further clarification 
of this definition is provided in
Regulation 18239 (copy enclosed). AIso enclosed is the FPPC
Information Manual on Lobbvincr Disclosure Provisions of the
Political 
Reform Act which explains the registration and reporting
requirements for lobbyists and lobbyist employers.
sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
ceneral counsel

sections.question_synthetic: What are the lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12 under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to issue formal advice because the requester is not authorized by the organization to seek advice, as the Commission cannot provide advice to third parties about the duties of another entity. General informational materials about lobbyist definitions and disclosure requirements are provided.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (informal advice request declined). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; content is embedded in letter body. Provides general informational guidance despite declining formal advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12 under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to issue formal advice because the requester is not authorized by the organization to seek advice, as the Commission cannot provide advice to third parties about the duties of another entity. General informational materials about lobbyist definitions and disclosure requirements are provided.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines John Kivi's request for informal advice regarding lobbying disclosure obligations of the American Federation of Musicians Local 12, explaining that it cannot advise third parties about another entity's obligations, but provides general information about lobbyist definitions and regulatory requirements.
